title: Garlicky Potato Skins
summary: We’ve packed garlicky potato skins with a healthy dose of green veg and draped them with a tangy cornichon-spiked salsa verde.

method.description:
Prick the potatoes all over with a sharp knife. Wrap the whole
garlic bulb in foil. Put the potatoes into a roasting tin, season and
bake for 1 hour until completely tender, adding the wrapped garlic
to the tin after 20 minutes. After 40 minutes it should be super soft.

Meanwhile, halve and thinly slice the leeks. Using one hand,
hold each cavolo nero stalk and use the other to strip the leaves
away from the stalk, then finely chop the stalks.

Get a large frying pan over a medium heat. Add 2 tsp of
the olive oil, the sliced leeks and chopped cavolo nero stalks along
with 3 tbsp of water. Cook until the water has evaporated
and the leeks are collapsed and soft. Add the cavolo nero leaves and
peas and cook for a few minutes more until everything is tender.
Stir through 2 tsp of the mustard, then scrape into a large
bowl and set aside.

Once the garlic is cooked, squeeze out onto a chopping board,
mash with a fork then stir through the greens.

Cut the baked potatoes in half and leave to cool a little, then
spoon the potato centre into the greens and mix together. Increase
the oven temperature to 220°C.

Put the potato skins back into the roasting tin, drizzle with a
teaspoon of olive oil and bake for 5 minutes so that they crisp
up slightly.

Meanwhile, grate the cheese. Season the filling to taste and then
load into the potato skins and sprinkle over the cheese. Return to
the oven for 10 minutes until bubbling and golden.

Salsa verde time. Chop the cornichons and parsley (stalks and all)
and mix together with the remaining teaspoon of mustard, 2 tbsp of olive oil and the red wine vinegar. Season to taste –
you want it punchy. Serve the cornichon salsa verde with the loaded
potato skins and a big green salad, if you like.

chefs.name: Mob
chefs.summary: Mob is on a mission to instil a love of cooking in everyone, because we believe in the positive impact that it can have on people's lives.
